title: Source lineage for ajf7399 / Richard Rolle's English Psalter
summary: The checked-in XML is a normalized repository version of University of Michigan CME item AJF7399.0001.001, a 2006 uncorrected OCR/Level-1 encoding of H. R. Bramley's 1884 English Psalter edition. U-M's 578-image sequence represents an unidentified printed copy from its title page through the glossary but is physically partial; the independently cached 624-page Robarts scan represents one more complete bound copy. Bramley's Middle English reading text uses University College MS 64 (U) as late-selected base, including later U-supply leaves; his Latin text and selected readings pass through a complete working transcript of Sidney Sussex MS 89 (S); Laud Misc. 286 (L) supplies the sixty-line metrical preface and six finite gaps. Eleven additional manuscripts enter at specimen or selected-apparatus scope. Direct inspection corrects three OCR-corrupted specimen headings to Tanner 1, Laud Misc. 321, and Magdalen College MS 52; one printed B 469 apparatus reference remains unresolved. Eleven pinned Oxford TEI catalog expressions, James's Sidney catalog, two scholarly articles, selected manuscript images, access routes, and rights layers are modeled separately. Public catalog access never implies manuscript-image access, and provider-inventory completeness never implies complete physical-carrier coverage.
review_notes: One cataloged medieval prose work contains a prose prologue, 150 Psalm divisions, and seven Canticle divisions. The anonymous sixty-line verse preface is zero-item edition paratext.
No normalized composition place is asserted; witness production, holding, dialect, and author association remain separate.
Every modeled entity has an explicit rights record. Catalog-expression rights are not attached to physical manuscript entities.

entities.label: General Corpus XML artifact ajf7399
entities.description: The checked-in XML serialization pinned through the CME submodule. It preserves U-M's OCR text and header while adding systematic NODE and note ID attributes and changing EDITORIALDECL/@N.

entities.label: University of Michigan CME item AJF7399.0001.001
entities.description: U-M's 2006 Level-1 TEI-in-Libraries electronic text, produced by uncorrected OCR from linked page images and not content-edited. It continuously transcribes the edition title, anonymous metrical preface, Rolle's prose prologue, Psalms and Canticles, while representing most introduction and glossary pages only by page milestones and omission placeholders.

entities.label: University of Michigan 578-image sequence for AJF7399.0001.001
entities.description: The raw Michigan Book ZIP's 578 unique contiguous bilevel Group-4 TIFFs, all 3296 by 5088 pixels, plus source XML, text, header, pageview data, and ingest log. The published sequence is complete at provider-inventory level but starts with the edition title page and omits covers, spine, early preliminaries, advertisements, and other carrier surfaces. The physical scan copy is unidentified.
entities.reproduction_format: 578 bilevel TIFF page images in a Raw Michigan Book ZIP
notes: Provider-inventory completeness is not physical-carrier completeness.
seq. 1 is the title page; seq. 23-24 the anonymous preface; seq. 25-548 Rolle's work; seq. 549-578 the glossary.

entities.label: English Psalter (abstract work)
alternate_labels: Commentary on the Psalter and Canticles
entities.description: Richard Rolle's one medieval prose work: Latin Psalm and Canticle lemmata, Middle English verse-by-verse translation, and Middle English prose commentary for 150 Psalms and seven Canticles. It is distinct from its witnesses, Bramley's edition, the U-M encoding, and the anonymous metrical preface.

entities.label: H. R. Bramley's 1884 edition of the English Psalter
entities.description: The Clarendon Press edition whose title matter, anonymous metrical preface on pp. 1-2, Rolle work on pp. 3-526, and selected editorial apparatus are represented by ajf7399. Bramley used U for the vernacular base, S for the Latin and comparison through a complete transcript, L for six gaps and the preface, and eleven other witnesses at specimen or selected-reading scope.

bibliographic.citation: Richard Rolle, The Psalter, or Psalms of David and Certain Canticles, with a Translation and Exposition in English, ed. H. R. Bramley (Oxford: Clarendon Press, 1884).
bibliographic.title: The Psalter, or Psalms of David and Certain Canticles

notes: The Robarts bound copy also contains 32 pages of publisher advertisements.

entities.label: Internet Archive complete Robarts scan of Bramley's 1884 edition
entities.description: A 624-page PDF reproduction of the Robarts copy: front cover, title/preliminaries, xxiv + 556-page volume, 32-page publisher advertisements, rear blanks, and back cover. Scandata excludes six delete/color-card/white-card leaves. No separate spine or edge capture is present.
entities.reproduction_format: 624-page PDF plus OCR and scan metadata

entities.label: Bramley's complete working transcript of Sidney Sussex MS 89
entities.description: A modern editorial process carrier prepared by Bramley before his late switch from S to U. Kuczynski confirms that Bramley transcribed all of S and used that material for the Latin text and selected vernacular readings. This is not a medieval witness; manuscript_witness is a schema compromise. Its current survival and location are unknown.

notes: Current carrier, extent, and repository are unknown.
Do not collapse this intermediary into the physical S codex or count it as a medieval witness.

entities.label: University College, Oxford, MS 64 (U)
entities.description: Bramley's late-selected vernacular base witness. The fifteenth-century paper codex contains Rolle's Commentary on the Psalms and Canticles in Middle English associated with Yorkshire dialect. Five spans are on late-sixteenth-century U-supply leaves; the later layer remains part of this physical codex.

physical_description.extent: Bramley says the codex originally consisted of 136 leaves

entities.label: Sidney Sussex College, Cambridge, MS 89 (S)
entities.description: Bramley's initially chosen base and the source of his complete working transcript. James describes a verse-for-verse English Rolle paraphrase with the Latin of each verse in large letters. Bramley used S for the Latin throughout and for selected vernacular readings.

date_statements.label: catalog/editorial dating
date_statements.value: 14th-15th century; Bramley says towards the close of the 14th century

holding.notes: No exact current item-level public catalog or facsimile was verified on 2026-07-16.

notes: Double columns of 36 lines; James dates cent. xiv-xv.

entities.label: Bodleian Library, MS Laud Misc. 286 (L)
entities.description: Bramley's source for the anonymous sixty-line metrical preface and six finite gap supplies. The current catalog dates the English parchment codex to the beginning of the fifteenth century.

entities.label: Bodleian Library, MS Tanner 1
entities.description: Bramley's specimen no. 4 and a narrowly cited apparatus comparison witness. Direct inspection corrects XML/OCR 'Tangier 1' to printed Tanner 1.

entities.label: Bodleian Library, MS Laud Misc. 321
entities.description: Bramley's specimen no. 5. Direct inspection corrects XML/OCR 'Laud 121' to the printed and current shelfmark Laud Misc. 321. No body-apparatus role beyond the specimens was demonstrated.

entities.label: Bodleian Library, MS Laud Misc. 448
entities.description: Bramley's specimen no. 6 and a narrowly cited apparatus comparison witness.

entities.label: Bodleian Library, MS Bodley 467
entities.description: Bramley's specimen no. 7 and a selected apparatus comparison witness. It contains Rolle's Commentary on the Psalter.

entities.label: Magdalen College, Oxford, MS 52 (M)
entities.description: Bramley's specimen no. 8 and a selected apparatus comparison witness. Direct inspection corrects XML/OCR 'Magdalen College MS 86' to printed MS 52. No exact current public item record or complete facsimile was verified.

holding.notes: Magdalen's improved Western-manuscript catalog is described as in preparation.

entities.label: University College, Oxford, MS 56 (U-double-prime)
entities.description: Bramley's specimen no. 9 and a selected apparatus comparison witness. XML OCR UII represents the printed siglum U-double-prime and must not become a witness name.

entities.label: Bodleian Library, MS Bodley 953
entities.description: Bramley's specimen no. 10 and a selected apparatus comparison witness. Current cataloging dates this parchment codex to the end of the fourteenth century and tentatively associates it with Bristol.

entities.label: Bodleian Library, MS Bodley 877
entities.description: Bramley's specimen no. 11 and a selected apparatus comparison witness. The current catalog describes an interpolated version of Rolle's Commentary on the Psalter and Canticles.

entities.label: Bodleian Library, MS Bodley 288
entities.description: Bramley's specimen no. 12 and a selected apparatus comparison witness. Current cataloging dates the parchment codex to the end of the fourteenth century; it represents an interpolated or Wycliffite-revision stream rather than U's original-version wording.

entities.label: Newcastle upon Tyne Public Library MS TH.1678 (N)
entities.description: Bramley's late-arriving specimen no. 13 and a narrowly used apparatus comparison witness. Breeze independently confirms the exact current-form shelfmark and English Psalter/Canticles content. Bramley reports numerous losses and damp/soil damage.

holding.notes: Special Collections, Level 6, City Library; no official item-level public record or facsimile was verified.

notes: Bramley says it begins at Psalm 39:1 and has multiple lacunae and illegible passages from dirt and damp.

entities.label: Bodleian Library, MS Tanner 16
entities.description: Bramley's specimen no. 14. No body-apparatus role beyond the specimens was demonstrated. Bramley describes a surviving part ending at Psalm 80:7.

entities.label: M. R. James, catalogue of Sidney Sussex manuscripts (1895)
entities.description: The complete printed catalog; entry MS 89 on printed p. 73 describes S's material, layout, date, contents, and Rolle attribution. The catalog is description, not a target-work transcription or manuscript facsimile.

bibliographic.citation: M. R. James, A Descriptive Catalogue of the Manuscripts in the Library of Sidney Sussex College, Cambridge (Cambridge: Cambridge University Press, 1895).
bibliographic.title: A Descriptive Catalogue of the Manuscripts in the Library of Sidney Sussex College, Cambridge

entities.label: Michael P. Kuczynski, study of Bramley and the English Psalter (2024)
entities.description: A complete scholarly article describing the work, Bramley edition, manuscript census, editorial process, S transcript, late base change to U, and U/S/L roles. It contains selected quotations/discussion and one selected U photograph, not a whole-work transcription or whole-manuscript facsimile.

bibliographic.citation: Michael P. Kuczynski, 'Henry Ramsden Bramley: His Life and Edition of Richard Rolle's English Psalter,' Roczniki Humanistyczne 72.5 special issue (2024), 67-86.
bibliographic.title: Henry Ramsden Bramley: His Life and Edition of Richard Rolle's English Psalter

entities.label: Andrew Breeze, study using Newcastle MS TH.1678 (1995)
entities.description: A complete five-page scholarly article confirming TH.1678's exact shelfmark and English Psalter/Canticles content and discussing selected target wording. It is scholarship with selected quotation/discussion, not a whole-work transcription or facsimile.

bibliographic.citation: Andrew Breeze, 'Richard Rolle's tagild, entangled: Welsh tagu, choke, tagell, snare,' SELIM 5.1 (1995), 132-136.
bibliographic.title: Richard Rolle's tagild, entangled: Welsh tagu, choke, tagell, snare

entities.label: U.S. Copyright Office Circular 15A, Duration of Copyright
entities.description: Official supporting legal guidance used only to evaluate United States copyright duration for the 1884 and 1895 publications. It contains no target-work portion.

bibliographic.citation: United States Copyright Office, Circular 15A, Duration of Copyright.
bibliographic.title: Duration of Copyright

entities.label: Kuczynski Figure 5: University College MS 64, fols. 74v-75r
entities.description: One published selected photograph spanning U fols. 74v-75r. Kuczynski identifies f. 74v as the fifteenth-century original layer and f. 75r as a late-sixteenth-century supply leaf. The figure is not a complete manuscript surrogate.
entities.reproduction_format: selected two-page manuscript photograph embedded in a scholarly PDF

entities.label: Digital Bodleian selected image of Laud Misc. 286
entities.description: The complete one-canvas provider selection, f. 1r, showing the opening of the anonymous metrical preface. It is physically partial.

entities.label: Digital Bodleian selected image of Laud Misc. 321
entities.description: The complete one-canvas provider selection, f. 1r, showing Rolle's prologue. It is physically partial.

entities.label: Digital Bodleian selected images of Laud Misc. 448
entities.description: The complete two-canvas provider selection, f. 1v Psalm 1 and f. 41r Psalm 38/39. Both show target work; the object is physically partial.

entities.label: Digital Bodleian selected images of Bodley 953
entities.description: The complete 23-canvas provider selection. Direct visual audit confirms all selected canvases show the target Psalter rather than the codex's calendar. It is physically partial.

entities.label: Digital Bodleian selected images of Tanner 16
entities.description: The complete three-canvas provider selection: f. 2v Psalm 1, f. 106v Psalm 38/39, and f. 233r Psalm 80/81. It is physically partial.

access.access_method: Search the Oxford University Press Archive, including file LB495, and Bramley papers for the reported complete S transcript.
Contact the University College Librarian for a visiting-scholar appointment or image enquiry; the manuscripts are housed in the Bodleian.
notes: No complete public MS 64 facsimile was found; Kuczynski Figure 5 is only a selected two-page image.

access.access_method: Submit a written Special Collections enquiry and arrange an appointment at least one week in advance.
notes: No complete public MS 89 facsimile was found; James's PDF is catalog evidence only.

rights.component: repository XML serialization and repository-added attributes
rights.basis: The pinned CME repository LICENSE dedicates 'software' under the Unlicense, but its applicability to the encoded source text and edition-derived content is not explicit.
notes: Do not infer that the repository license resolves rights in Bramley's edition, the medieval text, manuscript images, or U-M's encoding.

rights.component: current U-M digital encoding and OCR text
rights.basis: The current U-M item states that no known copyright restrictions apply in the United States.

rights.component: legacy electronic-text claim preserved in the 2006 TEI header
rights.basis: The source header preserves a 2006 Regents of the University of Michigan copyright statement alongside the current no-known-copyright access statement; the historical claim is recorded without treating it as a current restriction determination.

rights.component: published page-image sequence of the unidentified 1884 print copy
rights.basis: The provider associates the item with a U.S. no-known-copyright statement; the images reproduce a public-domain 1884 edition, but the exact scan carrier and contractual reuse terms are not independently resolved.

rights.component: medieval English Psalter work
rights.basis: The work was composed in the fourteenth century and its copyright term has long expired.

rights.component: 1884 editorial text, introduction, notes, and apparatus
rights.basis: The edition was published in 1884; U.S. Copyright Office Circular 15A explains that works published in the United States before 1930 are in the public domain as of 2026.

rights.component: Robarts/Internet Archive scan files and scan presentation
rights.basis: The underlying 1884 edition is public domain in the United States, but no item-specific license for the scan files was verified.

rights.component: Bramley's unpublished working transcript as an editorial expression
rights.basis: The transcript's survival, present location, publication history, and any applicable rights record are unknown.

rights.component: underlying medieval textual content embodied in the physical witness
rights.basis: The medieval text and fifteenth- and sixteenth-century scribal expressions are outside copyright term.
notes: This record does not grant access to the physical codex or license photographs of it.

rights.component: underlying medieval textual content embodied in the physical witness
rights.basis: The fourteenth- or fifteenth-century scribal expression is outside copyright term.
notes: This record does not license manuscript photography or waive repository conditions.

rights.basis: The early-fifteenth-century scribal expression is outside copyright term.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Multiple extant Rolle Psalter witnesses and an already completed transcript of S
editorial_practices.representation: U supplies the vernacular base; S supplies the Latin and selected readings; L and finite other witnesses supply documented exceptions and comparisons.
editorial_practices.description: Bramley changed his planned base late in the project from S to University College MS 64 (U), while retaining distinct S and L roles.
editorial_practices.scope: Rolle's prose work on printed pp. 3-526; the anonymous metrical preface is separately selected from L.

editorial_practices.source_feature: U-based vernacular text, complete S transcript, L gap supplies, and eleven further specimen or apparatus witnesses
editorial_practices.representation: Departures from the primary manuscript are reported in the foot-of-page apparatus, with witness scope varying from continuous S comparison to narrow specimen or selected-reading use.
editorial_practices.description: The edition collates U against S throughout and against L and a finite additional witness set at documented scopes; it does not establish full collation of every witness.
editorial_practices.scope: Printed pp. 3-526 and the fourteen specimen extracts in the introduction.

editorial_practices.source_feature: U's single points and inverted semicolons
editorial_practices.representation: Most manuscript stops are retained and supplemented by commas, colons, and occasional full stops.
editorial_practices.description: Bramley explicitly supplements rather than silently reproduces the base manuscript's punctuation.
editorial_practices.scope: The edited vernacular text based on U.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Bramley's complete printed volume and the provider's 578-image sequence
editorial_practices.representation: The title, anonymous preface, Rolle work, and apparatus are continuously OCR encoded; most introduction and glossary matter is represented only by page milestones and omission placeholders.
editorial_practices.description: Level-1 encoding coverage is selective even though the page-image sequence runs continuously from title page through glossary.
editorial_practices.scope: AJF7399.0001.001, especially DLPS23-24 for the preface, DLPS25-548 for Rolle's work, and DLPS549-578 for the mostly omitted glossary.

evidence.citation: General Corpus, CME/source/CME_phase_1-2/ajf7399.xml, CME commit e1d77c88ca561be924b2fedc54dd474be2e7e49c.
evidence.locator: TEI header and complete document
evidence.summary: Pins the 1,508,444-byte repository artifact, U-M identifiers, 1884 source edition, 2006 OCR/Level-1 encoding statement, rights language, work structure, page links, note apparatus, and repository-added attributes.

evidence.citation: Local namespace-aware structural audit of ajf7399.xml against U-M's raw-package source XML.
evidence.locator: 8,896-element sequence; DIV, NOTE, P, LG, L, PB, and EPB counts; normalized text and tail comparison
evidence.summary: Confirms one work-level root content part, a zero-item sixty-line metrical preface, one prose prologue, 150 Psalm divisions, seven Canticles, and repository normalization differences without textual divergence from the U-M raw XML.

evidence.citation: Kyvero Press CME repository, LICENSE at commit e1d77c88ca561be924b2fedc54dd474be2e7e49c.
evidence.locator: opening dedication and software scope
evidence.summary: The repository uses the Unlicense and explicitly calls the dedicated subject 'software'; it does not separately identify rights in each encoded text, edition, or image.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library, Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse, AJF7399.0001.001.
evidence.locator: item bibliographic header, table of contents, full-text and page-image views, access statement
evidence.summary: Confirms the U-M item identity, source edition, digital-text access, page-image linkage, and current no-known-copyright statement.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan / Internet Archive, AJF7399.0001.001 Raw Michigan Book package.
evidence.locator: 583 package members: 578 TIFFs plus XML, text, header, pageview data, and ingest log
evidence.summary: Provides the complete published provider inventory and the 1,398,386-byte U-M source XML used to distinguish the upstream encoding from the repository version.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive metadata API response for ajf7399.0001.001.umich.edu.
evidence.summary: Records the raw-package file inventory, sizes, checksums, item identifiers, and mirror metadata; it contains no target-work text itself.

evidence.citation: Local integrity and visual audit of the U-M Raw Michigan Book image sequence.
evidence.locator: all 578 TIFF members; seq. 1, 22-25, 548-578; first and last carrier surfaces
evidence.summary: Confirms 578 unique contiguous 3296-by-5088 bilevel Group-4 TIFFs, title-page start, work and glossary locators, provider-inventory completeness, physical-carrier partiality, and absence of an identifying ownership mark for the scan copy.

evidence.citation: Richard Rolle, The Psalter, or Psalms of David and Certain Canticles, with a Translation and Exposition in English, ed. H. R. Bramley (Oxford: Clarendon Press, 1884).
evidence.locator: title and copyright matter; introduction pp. xiii-xxiv; text pp. 1-526; glossary pp. 527-556
evidence.summary: Primary edition evidence for work boundaries, manuscript list, corrected specimen headings, U/S/L roles, finite L and U-supply spans, punctuation policy, collation, page locators, and the unresolved B 469 reference.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive scandata for psalterorpsalmso00roll.
evidence.locator: pageData leaf sequence and page-number assertions
evidence.summary: Distinguishes the 624 admitted PDF surfaces from six excluded delete/color-card/white-card leaves and maps preliminaries, printed pagination, advertisements, blanks, and covers.

evidence.citation: Local page-by-page boundary and specimen-heading audit of the Robarts 624-page PDF.
evidence.locator: PDF pp. 1-624; especially pp. 28-32 and printed introduction pp. xiii-xxiv
evidence.summary: Confirms the complete bound-copy sequence, six excluded scan leaves, absent separate spine/edges, and printed Tanner 1, Laud Misc. 321, and Magdalen College MS 52 headings where OCR is corrupt.

evidence.citation: M. R. James, A Descriptive Catalogue of the Manuscripts in the Library of Sidney Sussex College, Cambridge (Cambridge, 1895).
evidence.locator: MS 89 entry, printed p. 73
evidence.summary: Describes S as a 188-folio vellum Psalter manuscript, gives its historical shelfmark and layout, and attributes its English paraphrase to Rolle; the complete cached catalog contains no manuscript facsimile or work transcription.

evidence.citation: Michael P. Kuczynski, 'Henry Ramsden Bramley: His Life and Edition of Richard Rolle's English Psalter,' Roczniki Humanistyczne 72.5 special issue (2024), 67-86.
evidence.locator: complete article; especially printed pp. 76-85 and Figure 5
evidence.summary: Confirms the one-work Rolle boundary, Bramley's complete S transcript, late base change to U, distinct Latin and vernacular source roles, corrected witness census, U's later supply leaves, and the article's CC BY-NC-ND 4.0 license.

evidence.citation: Local inspection of Kuczynski 2024, Figure 5.
evidence.locator: PDF p. 17 / printed p. 83, Figure 5
evidence.summary: Confirms a single selected two-page photograph of University College MS 64 fols. 74v-75r, the original/supply-leaf distinction, University College credit, and physical partiality; the article-wide license is not treated as an independent manuscript-image license.

evidence.citation: Andrew Breeze, 'Richard Rolle's tagild, entangled: Welsh tagu, choke, tagell, snare,' SELIM 5.1 (1995), 132-136.
evidence.locator: complete five-page article
evidence.summary: Confirms the exact TH.1678 shelfmark and English Psalter/Canticles content, provides selected witness discussion rather than a whole transcription or facsimile, and states a CC BY-NC-ND 3.0 Spain license.
notes: Exact provider bytes retained despite recoverable qpdf linearization/shared-object hint warnings.

evidence.citation: Oxford Western Manuscripts, University College MS 64, pinned TEI manuscript_13045.
evidence.locator: complete TEI record; msContents, history, physical description, availability
evidence.summary: Confirms shelfmark, identifiers, date, paper support, Yorkshire association, Rolle content, summary-description CC0 license, Coxe-text public-domain mark, and separate manuscript-image permission language.

evidence.citation: Oxford Western Manuscripts, University College MS 56, pinned TEI manuscript_13038.
evidence.locator: complete TEI record; msContents, history, physical description, availability
evidence.summary: Confirms shelfmark, identifiers, early-fifteenth-century date, Rolle Psalter/Cantica content, summary-description CC0 license, Coxe-text public-domain mark, and separate manuscript-image permission language.

evidence.citation: Oxford Western Manuscripts, Bodleian MS Laud Misc. 286, pinned TEI manuscript_7099.
evidence.summary: Confirms the early-fifteenth-century parchment witness, Rolle Psalter content, and official record identity; the pinned expression contains no explicit catalog-text license.

evidence.citation: Oxford Western Manuscripts, Bodleian MS Laud Misc. 321, pinned TEI manuscript_7133.
evidence.summary: Confirms the early-fifteenth-century parchment witness, Rolle Psalter content, and official record identity; the pinned expression contains no explicit catalog-text license.

evidence.citation: Oxford Western Manuscripts, Bodleian MS Laud Misc. 448, pinned TEI manuscript_7249.
evidence.summary: Confirms the parchment witness, Rolle Psalter/Canticle content, and official record identity; the pinned expression contains no explicit catalog-text license.

evidence.citation: Oxford Western Manuscripts, Bodleian MS Tanner 1, pinned TEI manuscript_8964.
evidence.summary: Confirms Tanner 1's official shelfmark, early-fifteenth-century parchment carrier, Rolle content, and catalog identity; the pinned expression contains no explicit catalog-text license.

evidence.citation: Oxford Western Manuscripts, Bodleian MS Tanner 16, pinned TEI manuscript_8972.
evidence.summary: Confirms Tanner 16's official shelfmark, fifteenth-century partial Rolle Psalter carrier, and catalog identity; the pinned expression contains no explicit catalog-text license.

evidence.citation: Oxford Western Manuscripts, Bodleian MS Bodley 288, pinned TEI manuscript_1338.
evidence.summary: Confirms the late-fourteenth-century parchment codex, interpolated or Wycliffite-revision Rolle Psalter content, and official record identity; the pinned expression contains no explicit catalog-text license.

evidence.citation: Oxford Western Manuscripts, Bodleian MS Bodley 467, pinned TEI manuscript_1502.
evidence.summary: Confirms the fifteenth-century parchment codex, Rolle Commentary on the Psalter, extent, dimensions, and official record identity; the pinned expression contains no explicit catalog-text license.

evidence.citation: Oxford Western Manuscripts, Bodleian MS Bodley 877, pinned TEI manuscript_1866.
evidence.summary: Confirms the first-half-fifteenth-century parchment codex, interpolated Rolle Psalter/Canticles content, extent, dimensions, and official record identity; the pinned expression contains no explicit catalog-text license.

evidence.citation: Oxford Western Manuscripts, Bodleian MS Bodley 953, pinned TEI manuscript_1901.
evidence.summary: Confirms the late-fourteenth-century parchment codex, Rolle Psalter content, tentative Bristol association, and official record identity; the pinned expression contains no explicit catalog-text license.

evidence.citation: Magdalen College, Old Library and medieval manuscript catalog information.
evidence.summary: Confirms repository access context and that improved Western-manuscript cataloging is in preparation; no exact current public MS 52 item record or complete facsimile was verified.

evidence.citation: University College, Oxford, Library Collections and special-collections access information.
evidence.summary: Supports repository enquiry and appointment-based access routes for University College manuscripts; catalog availability is not treated as image access.

evidence.citation: Sidney Sussex College, Special Collections.
evidence.summary: Supports enquiry-based physical access to Sidney special collections; no exact public MS 89 item page or complete facsimile was verified.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, Special Collections manuscripts access information.
evidence.summary: Supports catalog, reading-room, and reproduction-enquiry distinctions for Bodleian witnesses; public MARCO records do not imply complete digital facsimiles.

evidence.citation: Newcastle Libraries, Newcastle Collection.
evidence.summary: Supports the City Library special-collections context and enquiry route; no official item-level TH.1678 record or complete facsimile was verified.

evidence.citation: United States Copyright Office, Circular 15A, Duration of Copyright.
evidence.locator: duration table for works first published in the United States
evidence.summary: Official supporting legal guidance for the U.S. public-domain determinations applied to the 1884 Bramley edition and 1895 James catalog; the circular contains no target-work portion.
notes: Exact provider bytes retained despite recoverable qpdf linearization hint warnings.

evidence.citation: Digital Bodleian, selected IIIF manifest for MS Laud Misc. 286.
evidence.locator: complete one-canvas provider selection, f. 1r
evidence.summary: Confirms a physically partial selected image showing the anonymous metrical preface, complete bundle inventory and member fixity, and Bodleian CC BY-NC 4.0 attribution terms.

evidence.citation: Digital Bodleian, selected IIIF manifest for MS Laud Misc. 321.
evidence.locator: complete one-canvas provider selection, f. 1r
evidence.summary: Confirms a physically partial selected image showing Rolle's prologue, complete bundle inventory and member fixity, and Bodleian CC BY-NC 4.0 attribution terms.

evidence.citation: Digital Bodleian, selected IIIF manifest for MS Laud Misc. 448.
evidence.locator: complete two-canvas provider selection, ff. 1v and 41r
evidence.summary: Confirms two physically partial target-work images, complete bundle inventory and member fixity, and Bodleian CC BY-NC 4.0 attribution terms.

evidence.citation: Digital Bodleian, selected IIIF manifest for MS Bodley 953.
evidence.locator: complete 23-canvas provider selection
evidence.summary: Confirms all 23 selected canvases visually contain the target Psalter rather than the codex calendar, while remaining physically partial; bundle member fixity and Bodleian CC BY-NC 4.0 attribution terms are preserved.

evidence.citation: Digital Bodleian, selected IIIF manifest for MS Tanner 16.
evidence.locator: complete three-canvas provider selection, ff. 2v, 106v, and 233r
evidence.summary: Confirms three physically partial target-work images spanning early, middle, and terminal surviving Psalter portions, complete bundle inventory and member fixity, and Bodleian CC BY-NC 4.0 attribution terms.

open_questions.question: Which physical copy of Bramley's 1884 edition was scanned for U-M item AJF7399.0001.001?
next_steps: Ask U-M Digital Content and Collections for digitization provenance or the source call number.
Compare any supplied provenance against copy-specific marks in the 578 TIFFs.
notes: The source XML's LC call number identifies the edition, not demonstrably the scanned carrier.

open_questions.question: Does Bramley's complete working transcript of Sidney Sussex MS 89 survive, and if so where?
next_steps: Search Bramley papers and institutional archives named in the biographical scholarship.
Ask Sidney Sussex and University College archives whether the transcript or related editorial papers are held.
notes: The transcript is kept as an unknown-survival intermediary rather than collapsed into S.

open_questions.question: What witness or source does Bramley's printed apparatus siglum 'B 469' identify?
next_steps: Audit every B 469 occurrence and surrounding sigla in the complete edition.
Compare Bramley's notes and contemporary Bodleian shelfmark systems before asserting an entity.
notes: No witness entity is created from the unresolved string.

open_questions.question: What is the exact current item-level public catalog record, current preferred shelfmark, and image availability for Sidney Sussex MS 89?
next_steps: Request a current catalog citation and shelfmark confirmation from Sidney Sussex Special Collections.
Ask whether any complete or selected digital facsimile exists.

open_questions.question: Can Magdalen College provide a current item-level description and digital-image inventory for MS 52?
next_steps: Contact Magdalen College Archives and Manuscripts for the forthcoming Western-manuscript catalog entry.
Verify Bramley's MS 52 identification against the current carrier before adding a catalog-expression entity.

open_questions.question: Can Newcastle City Library provide an official item-level catalog record, current condition statement, and image availability for TH.1678?
next_steps: Contact the Newcastle Collection at City Library.
Reconcile any current description with Bramley's loss/condition notes and Breeze's exact shelfmark.

open_questions.question: Did Bramley use Laud Misc. 321 or Tanner 16 anywhere beyond the fourteen introductory specimen extracts?
next_steps: Perform a siglum-by-siglum apparatus audit against the printed introduction and OCR variants.
Add collated_against relations only if unambiguous body readings are located.

open_questions.question: Can the often-repeated 1326-1327 composition dating for Rolle's English Psalter be established more securely?
next_steps: Review current Rolle scholarship and the textual arguments behind the proposed range.
Keep the work metadata's composition date uncertain unless the evidentiary chain is explicit.
notes: No normalized composition place is inferred from witness dialect, holding, or author biography.

open_questions.question: Does the CME repository's software-worded Unlicense intentionally apply to individual encoded text artifacts such as ajf7399.xml?
next_steps: Seek an explicit content-license statement from the repository owner.
Retain component-level source and image rights even if repository-code licensing is clarified.

open_questions.question: What reuse terms govern the nine pinned Bodleian catalog TEI expressions that contain no explicit availability element?
next_steps: Ask Bodleian Libraries whether a repository-wide metadata license covers these TEI records.
Do not transfer any catalog-expression answer to manuscript-image rights.

open_questions.question: What image-specific permission or license governs Kuczynski Figure 5's photograph of University College MS 64 fols. 74v-75r?
next_steps: Ask University College Library and the journal whether the figure is excluded from the article-wide CC license.
Treat reuse as permission-required until image-specific terms are supplied.

open_questions.question: How should U-M's 2006 Regents copyright statement be reconciled with the current NoC-US item statement for the encoding?
next_steps: Request U-M's current component-level rights rationale for the OCR, TEI encoding, and page images.
Preserve both statements as separate rights layers meanwhile.
